summaryText: Pastor Ryan Windauer opened by sharing a personal update about his family and his passion for the next generation, using the story of Jesus healing the demon-possessed man in Luke 8 as a template. He emphasized that Jesus loves the next generation and that this love should compel every believer to invest in them. The message highlighted that faith is not a personal sprint but a generational relay race, and we are currently living in a critical 'exchange zone' moment where the baton of faith must be passed. Pastor Ryan addressed the unique challenges and loneliness faced by younger generations, contrasting them with the values of older generations, and called the church to provide godly community and opportunities for genuine encounters with Jesus. He challenged parents to make church a priority and encouraged every believer, regardless of age or parental status, to see their legacy as investing in people, not just accumulating possessions. The sermon concluded with an invitation to surrender to Christ and a call to action to share our testimonies and intentionally disciple the next generation.
mainPoints: Jesus loves the next generation, and therefore, loving and investing in them is a mandate for every believer, not just specialized ministers.
Faith is a generational relay race, not a personal sprint; we are in an 'exchange zone' moment where we must intentionally pass the baton of faith to those coming after us.
The next generation is facing unique challenges of loneliness and spiritual oppression; they need godly community and life-changing encounters with Jesus, which the church is called to provide.

versesMentioned.text: They sailed to the region of the Gerasenes, which is across the lake from Galilee. When Jesus stepped ashore, he was met by a demon-possessed man from the town. For a long time this man had not worn clothes or lived in a house, but had lived in the tombs... When they came to Jesus, they found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting at Jesus’ feet, dressed and in his right mind; and they were afraid... So the man went away and told all over town how much Jesus had done for him.
One generation commends your works to another; they tell of your mighty acts.

Love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength. These commandments that I give you today are to be on your hearts. Impress them on your children. Talk about them when you sit at home and when you walk along the road, when you lie down and when you get up.
Then he said, 'I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.' At this, Moses hid his face, because he was afraid to look at God.
After that whole generation had been gathered to their ancestors, another generation grew up who knew neither the LORD nor what he had done for Israel.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 145:4 - 'One generation commends your works to another; they tell of your mighty acts.'
Monday.action: Identify one thing God has done for you and share it with someone from a different generation today.
Monday.prayer: God, give me the courage to share my story. Let my testimony be a bridge of faith to the next generation.

Tuesday.scripture: Deuteronomy 6:6-7 - 'These commandments... are to be on your hearts. Impress them on your children...'
Tuesday.action: Have a spiritual conversation with a young person in your life—a child, relative, or coworker—about God's goodness.
Tuesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, show me how to naturally and faithfully impress your truths on the hearts of the next generation.

Wednesday.scripture: Luke 8:39 - 'Return home and tell how much God has done for you.'
Wednesday.action: Write down the names of three people you are praying for to come to know Jesus or grow in their faith.
Wednesday.prayer: Lord, remind me that my greatest legacy is people. Use my life to point others to you.

Thursday.scripture: Exodus 3:6 - 'I am the God of your father, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.'
Thursday.action: Reach out to an older believer to thank them for their faith or to a younger believer to encourage them.
Thursday.prayer: God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, thank you for your faithfulness across generations. Help me to connect and learn across generational lines in your family.

Friday.scripture: Judges 2:10 - '...another generation grew up who knew neither the LORD nor what he had done...'
Friday.focus: The Urgency of the Exchange Zone
Friday.action: Evaluate one area of your life (e.g., a habit, attitude, or unresolved hurt) and ask God for healing so it isn't passed on.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I don't want my unresolved issues to become a generational problem. Heal me and help me break cycles for the sake of those who come after me.

Saturday.scripture: Luke 8:35 - '...they found the man... sitting at Jesus’ feet, dressed and in his right mind...'
Saturday.action: Pray for the youth, young adult, and kids ministries at your church. Consider how you can support or serve them.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I pray for the next generation in our church. Provide them with life-changing encounters with you and a strong, godly community where they are known and loved.
